summary: Postinstall fetches and executes a native binary from an unclaimed placeholder GitHub account
details: scripts/postinstall.js unconditionally downloads a platform-specific binary from `https://github.com/yourusername/aora/releases/download/v0.1.0/<asset>`, writes it to `bin/aora`, and `chmod 0o755`s it on every install. `yourusername` is a literal placeholder string left in the source — it does not match the package publisher (`toni77777`). Anyone who registers or controls the `yourusername` GitHub account can publish releases at that path and have arbitrary native code delivered to every installer of this package. No hash or signature verification is performed on the downloaded bytes, and the hardcoded release tag (`v0.1.0`) does not even match the published package version (0.1.1). The resulting binary is later invoked through the package's CLI entry point. This is a hijackable install-time code-execution channel regardless of the author's intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
